contractSurface.securityClassification: tenant-scoped advertising consent, auction, fraud, and policy-review controls
coreGuarantees: Consent check runs first and blocks ad delivery when consent is missing, revoked, or expired.
Auction bids use SETNX for duplicate prevention before budget deduction.
Advertiser budget is decremented atomically and restored with INCRBY on rejection.
Auction audit records are append-only evidence, not mutable auction state.
Fraud detection runs before billing and restores budget on fraud rejection.
Payment evidence uses tokenized payment method references only; raw card number and CVV are never stored.
Political content review uses two independent model scores and conservative Math.min convergence.
Divergent political scores escalate to a human reviewer; ambiguous cases are not auto-blocked.
Every state event preserves store-before-enqueue ordering.
Tenant context resolution remains automatic through TenantContextResolver.

packageSigningConstraint.reason: FLOW-20 controls advertising consent, spend, fraud, and political content decisions, so Tier 3 requires package-signing first pass.

machineLockedRules: consent check ORDER 1 before auction processing
missing, revoked, or expired consent emits ConsentGateFailed
SETNX bid lock before bid processing
DECRBY budget deduction before accepted bid event
fraud audit stores the score used for the decision
no auto-block on ambiguous political cases

configurationKeys.description: Tenant-editable FLOW-20 operating policy values. These values may vary by tenant without changing consent-first delivery, Redis bid atomicity, fraud-before-billing, PCI zero-PAN, dual-model political review, human escalation, event ordering, or tenant isolation.

keys.semantic: Maximum time to wait for consent lookup before treating the ad delivery request as not authorized.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune latency tolerance while consent remains the first mandatory gate.

keys.semantic: Time-to-live for one auction bid duplicate-prevention lock.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune auction dedupe tolerance while SETNX remains mandatory.

keys.semantic: Maximum response window for synchronous auction bid processing.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune bid responsiveness while budget atomicity and audit ordering stay fixed.

keys.semantic: Fraud score at or above which a bid is rejected before billing.

boundedRange.rationale: Operators may tune fraud sensitivity while fraud still runs before billing and budget is restored on rejection.

keys.semantic: Retention window for fraud audit records.

boundedRange.rationale: Operators may tune investigation history while PCI zero-PAN and score auditability stay fixed.

keys.semantic: Conservative Math.min score above which content is treated as political when model agreement is strong.

boundedRange.rationale: Operators may tune political-content sensitivity while dual-model scoring and human escalation stay fixed.

keys.semantic: Maximum allowed difference between political model scores before mandatory human review.

boundedRange.rationale: Operators may tune ambiguity tolerance while divergent model outputs always route to human review.

keys.semantic: Expected completion window for human review of ambiguous political content.

boundedRange.rationale: Operators may tune review operations while ambiguous political content remains human-reviewed.

businessDomainAdaptation.description: Safe tenant adaptations are limited to advertising policy thresholds, auction latency posture, fraud sensitivity, political-review operations, and customer-facing module naming.

edgeCases.name: Strict privacy consent
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A uses a short consent lookup window and blocks delivery quickly when consent is unavailable.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T625 still checks consent first and emits ConsentGateFailed for missing, revoked, or expired consent.

edgeCases.name: High-volume auction tuning
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A adjusts bid lock and response timing for high-volume sponsored slots.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T626 still uses SETNX, atomic budget deduction, budget restoration, and append-only audit evidence.

edgeCases.name: Fraud-sensitive billing
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A lowers fraud tolerance and keeps fraud audit evidence longer.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T627 still evaluates fraud before billing, restores budget on rejection, and preserves PCI zero-PAN payloads.

edgeCases.name: Political review operations
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A tunes political detection thresholds and human review SLA.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T628 still uses two models, Math.min convergence, and mandatory human review for divergent scores.

ironRulesMACHINE.description: Fixed invariants that tenant adaptation cannot change.
rules: Task IDs T625 through T628 remain fixed.
Factory IDs F1561 through F1568 remain fixed.
Provider SDK access remains behind fabric interfaces.
Vault credential paths remain outside the package and outside tenant docs.
Consent check remains ORDER 1 before auction processing.
Missing, revoked, or expired consent always blocks ad delivery.
Rejected bids restore budget before rejection is emitted.
Auction and fraud audit records remain append-only.
Payment evidence remains tokenized; raw card number and CVV are not stored.
Political content detection uses at least two independent model scores.
Political convergence uses Math.min conservative consensus.
Divergent political scores require human review.
Ambiguous political cases are not auto-blocked.
All state events preserve store-before-enqueue ordering.
Tenant context resolution remains automatic through TenantContextResolver.

tenantAStrictPrivacyAds.description: Tenant A tunes Ads Platform for strict privacy posture and conservative campaign review.
tenantAStrictPrivacyAds.impact: Tenant A gets stricter consent, fraud, and political review posture while consent-first gating, Redis bid atomicity, budget restoration, PCI safety, human review, and tenant isolation stay unchanged.
tenantAStrictPrivacyAds.testable: Fork CI and live auth checks still pass after tenant.config.json and freedom-config.defaults.json record these values.

notPartOfAdaptationSurface.description: Elements that must not change during tenant adaptation.

passCriteria: Tenant adaptations may change only declared FLOW-20 configuration values or display/module metadata.
All four services remain MicroserviceBase subclasses and use TenantContextResolver for tenant scope.
Auth checks still return 401 for missing or cross-tenant token and 403 for wrong-role token after adaptation.
Forked package CI must pass npm install, TypeScript, Jest, and provenance steps.
